narrative:
DRIVER
USD leads D1 CSM at 100 in a Risk-Off High regime, driven by Iran geopolitical tensions and US 10Y yields surging to 4.655%, but the critical override is USD H4 CSM fading to 57 versus D1 100, a 43-point divergence flagged as the most important short-term signal. AUDUSD H4 Strong Sell at -6 with AUDJPY printing identical -6 and their +0.93 correlation confirming the cluster makes short AUDUSD and short AUDJPY the cleanest expressions, while USDCHF H4 Strong Buy at +6 with CHF H4 CSM collapsing to 0 from D1 68 adds a second high-conviction long. GBP H4 CSM accelerating to 100 versus D1 59 directly conflicts with the bearish GBP news theme, treat GBPUSD and GBPJPY longs as live tactical risk until H4 momentum reverses.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: GBPJPY H4 momentum 76 with NZDJPY accelerating on both TFs, both above 55
Fading: None

WATCH
Fed speaker on rate path hits wires, USDJPY directional acceleration confirms USD H4 fade or reversal
WTI Oil extends below 104, USDCAD D1 Buy at +4 faces bearish CAD energy support unwinding
US labor data release, EURUSD H4 Strong Sell at -5 triggers continuation toward D1 neutral resolution

themes.theme: US Dollar strength from Iran geopolitical tensions and hawkish Fed expectations
US Treasury yields surge to multi-year highs, pressuring risk assets
Euro weakness amid strong US data and divergent monetary policy
British Pound decline from higher US yields and domestic labor market softening
Canadian Dollar mixed signals from soft domestic CPI but energy support
Safe-haven flows into USD while equities face headwinds from rising rates
New Zealand Dollar weakness as USD outperforms on Fed hawkishness
Oil market volatility from Iran uncertainty offsetting supply concern concerns

key_observation: US Dollar Index hitting one-month highs driven by confluence of geopolitical risk premium from Iran tensions, hawkish Fed rate expectations, and Treasury yields surging to July 2007 levels. ADP employment beat reinforces inflation concerns. Risk-off sentiment benefiting USD as safe-haven while equities decline on rising rates. Euro, GBP, and NZD all weakening significantly against strengthening dollar.
watch: Iran military escalation trajectory and implications for oil supply. US Treasury yield sustainability above 5.2 percent on 30-year tenor and Fed's reaction function. ECB and BoE policy divergence with USD. Equity market capitulation levels given significant rate headwinds. USD strength persistence if geopolitical de-escalation occurs.
